verses.latin_vulgate: Profectus ergo Jacob venit in terram orientalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֥א יַעֲקֹ֖ב רַגְלָ֑יו וַיֵּ֖לֶךְ אַ֥רְצָה בְנֵי־קֶֽדֶם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Jacob went on in his journey, and came into the east country.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Jakob läksi matkaan: ja tuli itäiselle maalle.
verses.king_james: Then Jacob went on his journey, and came into the land of the people of the east.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vidit puteum in agro, tres quoque greges ovium accubantes juxta eum : nam ex illo adaquabantur pecora, et os ejus grandi lapide claudebatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֞רְא וְהִנֵּ֧ה בְאֵ֣ר בַּשָּׂדֶ֗ה וְהִנֵּה־שָׁ֞ם שְׁלֹשָׁ֤ה עֶדְרֵי־צֹאן֙ רֹבְצִ֣ים עָלֶ֔יהָ כִּ֚י מִן־הַבְּאֵ֣ר הַהִ֔וא יַשְׁק֖וּ הָעֲדָרִ֑ים וְהָאֶ֥בֶן גְּדֹלָ֖ה עַל־פִּ֥י הַבְּאֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he saw a well in the field, and three flocks of sheep lying by it: for the beasts were watered out of it, and the mouth thereof was closed with a great stone.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katseli ympärillensä, ja katso, kaivo oli kedolla. Ja katso, siellä makasi myös kolme lammaslaumaa sen tykönä, sillä siitä kaivosta juottivat he laumat: ja suuri kivi oli kaivon suulla.
verses.king_james: And he looked, and behold a well in the field, and, lo, there were three flocks of sheep lying by it; for out of that well they watered the flocks: and a great stone was upon the well’s mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Morisque erat ut cunctis ovibus congregatis devolverent lapidem, et refectis gregibus rursum super os putei ponerent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנֶאֶסְפוּ־שָׁ֣מָּה כׇל־הָעֲדָרִ֗ים וְגָלְל֤וּ אֶת־הָאֶ֙בֶן֙ מֵעַל֙ פִּ֣י הַבְּאֵ֔ר וְהִשְׁק֖וּ אֶת־הַצֹּ֑אן וְהֵשִׁ֧יבוּ אֶת־הָאֶ֛בֶן עַל־פִּ֥י הַבְּאֵ֖ר לִמְקֹמָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the custom was, when all the sheep were gathered together, to roll away the stone, and after the sheep were watered, to put it on the mouth of the well again.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä sinne koottiin kaikki laumat, ja vieritettiin kivi kaivon suulta, ja he juottivat lampaita: ja vierittivät kiven jälleen siallensa kaivon suulle.
verses.king_james: And thither were all the flocks gathered: and they rolled the stone from the well’s mouth, and watered the sheep, and put the stone again upon the well’s mouth in his place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ad pastores : Fratres, unde estis ? Qui responderunt : De Haran.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָהֶם֙ יַעֲקֹ֔ב אַחַ֖י מֵאַ֣יִן אַתֶּ֑ם וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ מֵחָרָ֖ן אֲנָֽחְנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to the shepherds: Brethren, whence are you? They answered: Of Haran.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob sanoi heille: minun veljeni, kusta te olette? he vastasivat: Haranista me olemme.
verses.king_james: And Jacob said unto them, My brethren, whence be ye? And they said, Of Haran are we.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quos interrogans, Numquid, ait, nostis Laban filium Nachor ? Dixerunt : Novimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָהֶ֔ם הַיְדַעְתֶּ֖ם אֶת־לָבָ֣ן בֶּן־נָח֑וֹר וַיֹּאמְר֖וּ יָדָֽעְנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he asked them, saying: Know you Laban, the son of Nachor? They said: We know him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi heille: tunnetteko Labanin, Nahorin pojan? he sanoivat: tunnemme kyllä.
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Know ye Laban the son of Nahor? And they said, We know him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sanusne est ? inquit. Valet, inquiunt : et ecce Rachel filia ejus venit cum grege suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר לָהֶ֖ם הֲשָׁל֣וֹם ל֑וֹ וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ שָׁל֔וֹם וְהִנֵּה֙ רָחֵ֣ל בִּתּ֔וֹ בָּאָ֖ה עִם־הַצֹּֽאן׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said: Is he in health? He is in health, say they: and behold, Rachel, his daughter, cometh with his flock.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: onko hän rauhassa? he vastasivat: rauhassa hän on. Ja katso, Rakel hänen tyttärensä tulee lammasten kanssa.
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Is he well? And they said, He is well: and, behold, Rachel his daughter cometh with the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Jacob : Adhuc multum diei superest, nec est tempus ut reducantur ad caulas greges : date ante potum ovibus, et sic eas ad pastum reducite.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הֵ֥ן עוֹד֙ הַיּ֣וֹם גָּד֔וֹל לֹא־עֵ֖ת הֵאָסֵ֣ף הַמִּקְנֶ֑ה הַשְׁק֥וּ הַצֹּ֖אן וּלְכ֥וּ רְעֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob said: There is yet much day remaining, neither is it time to bring the flocks into the folds again: first give the sheep drink, and so lead them back to feed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän sanoi: katso, vielä on paljo päivää, eikä vielä ole aika karjaa koota: niin juottakaat siis lampaita, menkäät ja kaitkaat heitä.
verses.king_james: And he said, Lo, it is yet high day, neither is it time that the cattle should be gathered together: water ye the sheep, and go and feed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui responderunt : Non possumus, donec omnia pecora congregentur, et amoveamus lapidem de ore putei, ut adaquemus greges.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְרוּ֮ לֹ֣א נוּכַל֒ עַ֣ד אֲשֶׁ֤ר יֵאָֽסְפוּ֙ כׇּל־הָ֣עֲדָרִ֔ים וְגָֽלְלוּ֙ אֶת־הָאֶ֔בֶן מֵעַ֖ל פִּ֣י הַבְּאֵ֑ר וְהִשְׁקִ֖ינוּ הַצֹּֽאן׃
verses.douay_rheims: They answered: We cannot, till all the cattle be gathered together, and we remove the stone from the well's mouth, that we may water the flocks.
verses.finnish_biblia: He sanoivat: emme saa siihenasti kuin kaikki laumat kootaan, ja vieritämme kiven kaivon suulta, juottaaksemme lampaat.
verses.king_james: And they said, We cannot, until all the flocks be gathered together, and till they roll the stone from the well’s mouth; then we water the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc loquebantur, et ecce Rachel veniebat cum ovibus patris sui : nam gregem ipsa pascebat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עוֹדֶ֖נּוּ מְדַבֵּ֣ר עִמָּ֑ם וְרָחֵ֣ל ׀ בָּ֗אָה עִם־הַצֹּאן֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְאָבִ֔יהָ כִּ֥י רֹעָ֖ה הִֽוא׃
verses.douay_rheims: They were yet speaking, and behold Rachel came with her father's sheep; for she fed the flock.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vielä hänen puhuissansa heidän kanssansa, tuli Rakel isänsä lammasten kanssa: sillä hän kaitsi lampaita.
verses.king_james: And while he yet spake with them, Rachel came with her father’s sheep: for she kept them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam cum vidisset Jacob, et sciret consobrinam suam, ovesque Laban avunculi sui, amovit lapidem quo puteus claudebatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֡י כַּאֲשֶׁר֩ רָאָ֨ה יַעֲקֹ֜ב אֶת־רָחֵ֗ל בַּת־לָבָן֙ אֲחִ֣י אִמּ֔וֹ וְאֶת־צֹ֥אן לָבָ֖ן אֲחִ֣י אִמּ֑וֹ וַיִּגַּ֣שׁ יַעֲקֹ֗ב וַיָּ֤גֶל אֶת־הָאֶ֙בֶן֙ מֵעַל֙ פִּ֣י הַבְּאֵ֔ר וַיַּ֕שְׁקְ אֶת־צֹ֥אן לָבָ֖ן אֲחִ֥י אִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Jacob saw her, and knew her to be his cousin german, and that they were the sheep of Laban, his uncle: he removed the stone wherewith the well was closed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui, koska Jakob näki Rakelin enonsa Labanin tyttären, ja enonsa Labanin lampaat: niin meni Jakob hänen tykönsä, ja vieritti kiven kaivon suulta. Ja juotti enonsa Labanin lampaat.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mother’s brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother’s brother, that Jacob went near, and rolled the stone from the well’s mouth, and watered the flock of Laban his mother’s brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et adaquato grege, osculatus est eam : et elevata voce flevit,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשַּׁ֥ק יַעֲקֹ֖ב לְרָחֵ֑ל וַיִּשָּׂ֥א אֶת־קֹל֖וֹ וַיֵּֽבְךְּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And having watered the flock, he kissed her: and lifting up his voice wept.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob suuta antoi Rakelin: ja korotti äänensä ja itki.
verses.king_james: And Jacob kissed Rachel, and lifted up his voice, and wept.

verses.latin_vulgate: et indicavit ei quod frater esset patris sui, et filius Rebeccæ : at illa festinans nuntiavit patri suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּגֵּ֨ד יַעֲקֹ֜ב לְרָחֵ֗ל כִּ֣י אֲחִ֤י אָבִ֙יהָ֙ ה֔וּא וְכִ֥י בֶן־רִבְקָ֖ה ה֑וּא וַתָּ֖רׇץ וַתַּגֵּ֥ד לְאָבִֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he told her that he was her father's brother, and the son of Rebecca: but she went in haste and told her father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ilmoitti hänelle, että hän oli hänen isänsä veli, Rebekan poika. Niin juoksi hän ja ilmoitti sen isällensä.
verses.king_james: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father’s brother, and that he was Rebekah’s son: and she ran and told her father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum audisset venisse Jacob filium sororis suæ, cucurrit obviam ei : complexusque eum, et in oscula ruens, duxit in domum suam. Auditis autem causis itineris,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִי֩ כִשְׁמֹ֨עַ לָבָ֜ן אֶת־שֵׁ֣מַע ׀ יַעֲקֹ֣ב בֶּן־אֲחֹת֗וֹ וַיָּ֤רׇץ לִקְרָאתוֹ֙ וַיְחַבֶּק־לוֹ֙ וַיְנַשֶּׁק־ל֔וֹ וַיְבִיאֵ֖הוּ אֶל־בֵּית֑וֹ וַיְסַפֵּ֣ר לְלָבָ֔ן אֵ֥ת כׇּל־הַדְּבָרִ֖ים הָאֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who, when he heard that Jacob his sister's son was come, ran forth to meet him: and embracing him, and heartily kissing him, brought him into his house. And when he had heard the causes of his journey,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska Laban kuuli sanoman Jakobista sisarensa pojasta, juoksi hän häntä vastaan, ja otti hänen syliinsä, ja suuta antoi hänen, ja vei hänen huoneesensa: ja hän jutteli Labanille kaiken asian.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Laban heard the tidings of Jacob his sister’s son, that he ran to meet him, and embraced him, and kissed him, and brought him to his house. And he told Laban all these things.

verses.latin_vulgate: respondit : Os meum es, et caro mea. Et postquam impleti sunt dies mensis unius,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ לָבָ֔ן אַ֛ךְ עַצְמִ֥י וּבְשָׂרִ֖י אָ֑תָּה וַיֵּ֥שֶׁב עִמּ֖וֹ חֹ֥דֶשׁ יָמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: He answered: Thou art my bone and my flesh. And after the days of one month were expired,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Laban hänelle: tosin sinä olet minun luuni ja lihani. Ja hän oli hänen tykönänsä koko kuukauden.
verses.king_james: And Laban said to him, Surely thou art my bone and my flesh. And he abode with him the space of a month.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit ei : Num quia frater meus es, gratis servies mihi ? dic quid mercedis accipias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָבָן֙ לְיַעֲקֹ֔ב הֲכִי־אָחִ֣י אַ֔תָּה וַעֲבַדְתַּ֖נִי חִנָּ֑ם הַגִּ֥ידָה לִּ֖י מַה־מַּשְׂכֻּרְתֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said to him: Because thou art my brother, shalt thou serve me without wages? Tell me what wages thou wilt have.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte sanoi Laban Jakobille: vaikkas olet minun veljeni, palveletkos sentähden minua ilman mitäkään? ilmoita minulle, mikä sinun palkkas olis.
verses.king_james: And Laban said unto Jacob, Because thou art my brother, shouldest thou therefore serve me for nought? tell me, what shall thy wages be?

verses.latin_vulgate: Habebat vero duas filias : nomen majoris Lia, minor vero appellabatur Rachel.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּלְלָבָ֖ן שְׁתֵּ֣י בָנ֑וֹת שֵׁ֤ם הַגְּדֹלָה֙ לֵאָ֔ה וְשֵׁ֥ם הַקְּטַנָּ֖ה רָחֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now he had two daughters, the name of the elder was Lia; and the younger was called Rachel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Labanilla oli kaksi tytärtä: vanhemman nimi oli Lea, ja nuoremman nimi oli Rakel.
verses.king_james: And Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed Lia lippis erat oculis : Rachel decora facie, et venusto aspectu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעֵינֵ֥י לֵאָ֖ה רַכּ֑וֹת וְרָחֵל֙ הָֽיְתָ֔ה יְפַת־תֹּ֖אַר וִיפַ֥ת מַרְאֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Lia was blear-eyed: Rachel was well favoured, and of a beautiful countenance.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Lea oli pehmiä silmistä; vaan Rakel oli kauniin muotoinen, ja ihana kasvoilta.
verses.king_james: Leah was tender eyed; but Rachel was beautiful and well favoured.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam diligens Jacob, ait : Serviam tibi pro Rachel filia tua minore, septem annis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֶּאֱהַ֥ב יַעֲקֹ֖ב אֶת־רָחֵ֑ל וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אֶֽעֱבׇדְךָ֙ שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֔ים בְּרָחֵ֥ל בִּתְּךָ֖ הַקְּטַנָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob being in love with her, said: I will serve thee seven years for Rachel, thy younger daughter.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob rakasti Rakelia: ja sanoi: minä palvelen sinua seitsemän ajastaikaa Rakelin, sinun nuoremman tyttäres tähden.
verses.king_james: And Jacob loved Rachel; and said, I will serve thee seven years for Rachel thy younger daughter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Laban : Melius est ut tibi eam dem quam alteri viro : mane apud me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָבָ֗ן ט֚וֹב תִּתִּ֣י אֹתָ֣הּ לָ֔ךְ מִתִּתִּ֥י אֹתָ֖הּ לְאִ֣ישׁ אַחֵ֑ר שְׁבָ֖ה עִמָּדִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Laban answered: It is better that I give her to thee than to another man; stay with me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laban vastasi: parempi on, että minä hänen annan sinulle, kuin jollekin toiselle: ole minun tykönäni.
verses.king_james: And Laban said, It is better that I give her to thee, than that I should give her to another man: abide with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servivit ergo Jacob pro Rachel septem annis : et videbantur illi pauci dies præ amoris magnitudine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲבֹ֧ד יַעֲקֹ֛ב בְּרָחֵ֖ל שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֑ים וַיִּהְי֤וּ בְעֵינָיו֙ כְּיָמִ֣ים אֲחָדִ֔ים בְּאַהֲבָת֖וֹ אֹתָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel: and they seemed but a few days, because of the greatness of his love.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin palveli Jakob Rakelin tähden seitsemän ajastaikaa: ja ne olivat hänen silmissänsä niinkuin muutamat päivät, rakastaissansa häntä.
verses.king_james: And Jacob served seven years for Rachel; and they seemed unto him but a few days, for the love he had to her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ad Laban : Da mihi uxorem meam : quia jam tempus impletum est, ut ingrediar ad illam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֤ב אֶל־לָבָן֙ הָבָ֣ה אֶת־אִשְׁתִּ֔י כִּ֥י מָלְא֖וּ יָמָ֑י וְאָב֖וֹאָה אֵלֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to Laban: Give me my wife; for now the time is fulfilled, that I may go in unto her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob sanoi Labanille: anna minun emäntäni minulle: sillä minun aikani on täytetty mennä hänen tykönsä.
verses.king_james: And Jacob said unto Laban, Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in unto her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui vocatis multis amicorum turbis ad convivium, fecit nuptias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֶּאֱסֹ֥ף לָבָ֛ן אֶת־כׇּל־אַנְשֵׁ֥י הַמָּק֖וֹם וַיַּ֥עַשׂ מִשְׁתֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he, having invited a great number of his friends to the feast, made the marriage.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Laban kutsui kokoon kaiken sen paikkakunnan miehet, ja piti häät.
verses.king_james: And Laban gathered together all the men of the place, and made a feast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vespere Liam filiam suam introduxit ad eum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י בָעֶ֔רֶב וַיִּקַּח֙ אֶת־לֵאָ֣ה בִתּ֔וֹ וַיָּבֵ֥א אֹתָ֖הּ אֵלָ֑יו וַיָּבֹ֖א אֵלֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And at night he brought in Lia, his daughter, to him,
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta ehtoona otti hän tyttärensä Lean, ja vei sen hänen tykönsä: ja hän makasi sen tykönä.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter, and brought her to him; and he went in unto her.

verses.latin_vulgate: dans ancillam filiæ, Zelpham nomine. Ad quam cum ex more Jacob fuisset ingressus, facto mane vidit Liam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֤ן לָבָן֙ לָ֔הּ אֶת־זִלְפָּ֖ה שִׁפְחָת֑וֹ לְלֵאָ֥ה בִתּ֖וֹ שִׁפְחָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Giving his daughter a handmaid, named Zelpha. Now when Jacob had gone in to her according to custom, when morning was come he saw it was Lia.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Laban antoi tyttärellensä Lealle piikansa Silpan piiaksi.
verses.king_james: And Laban gave unto his daughter Leah Zilpah his maid for an handmaid.

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit ad socerum suum : Quid est quod facere voluisti ? nonne pro Rachel servivi tibi ? quare imposuisti mihi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י בַבֹּ֔קֶר וְהִנֵּה־הִ֖וא לֵאָ֑ה וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֶל־לָבָ֗ן מַה־זֹּאת֙ עָשִׂ֣יתָ לִּ֔י הֲלֹ֤א בְרָחֵל֙ עָבַ֣דְתִּי עִמָּ֔ךְ וְלָ֖מָּה רִמִּיתָֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to his father-in-law: What is it that thou didst mean to do? did not I serve thee for Rachel? why hast thou deceived me?
verses.finnish_biblia: Aamulla, katso, se oli Lea. Ja hän sanoi Labanille: miksis tämän minulle teit? enkö minä Rakelin tähden palvellut sinua? miksis minun petit?
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that in the morning, behold, it was Leah: and he said to Laban, What is this thou hast done unto me? did not I serve with thee for Rachel? wherefore then hast thou beguiled me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Laban : Non est in loco nostro consuetudinis, ut minores ante tradamus ad nuptias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָבָ֔ן לֹא־יֵעָשֶׂ֥ה כֵ֖ן בִּמְקוֹמֵ֑נוּ לָתֵ֥ת הַצְּעִירָ֖ה לִפְנֵ֥י הַבְּכִירָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Laban answered: It is not the custom in this place, to give the younger in marriage first.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vastasi Laban: ei niin ole meidän maan tapa, että nuorempi annetaan ennen vanhempaa.
verses.king_james: And Laban said, It must not be so done in our country, to give the younger before the firstborn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Imple hebdomadam dierum hujus copulæ : et hanc quoque dabo tibi pro opere quo serviturus es mihi septem annis aliis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַלֵּ֖א שְׁבֻ֣עַ זֹ֑את וְנִתְּנָ֨ה לְךָ֜ גַּם־אֶת־זֹ֗את בַּעֲבֹדָה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר תַּעֲבֹ֣ד עִמָּדִ֔י ע֖וֹד שֶֽׁבַע־שָׁנִ֥ים אֲחֵרֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Make up the week of days of this match: and I will give thee her also, for the service that thou shalt render me other seven years.
verses.finnish_biblia: Täytä tämän viikko; niin se myös sinulle annetaan, siitä palveluksesta kun sinä palvelet minua vielä toiset seitsemän ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: Fulfil her week, and we will give thee this also for the service which thou shalt serve with me yet seven other years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Acquievit placito : et hebdomada transacta, Rachel duxit uxorem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַשׂ יַעֲקֹב֙ כֵּ֔ן וַיְמַלֵּ֖א שְׁבֻ֣עַ זֹ֑את וַיִּתֶּן־ל֛וֹ אֶת־רָחֵ֥ל בִּתּ֖וֹ ל֥וֹ לְאִשָּֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He yielded to his pleasure: and after the week was past, he married Rachel:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jakob teki niin, ja täytti hänen viikkonsa: ja hän antoi hänelle tyttärensä Rakelin emännäksi.
verses.king_james: And Jacob did so, and fulfilled her week: and he gave him Rachel his daughter to wife also.

verses.latin_vulgate: cui pater servam Balam tradiderat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֤ן לָבָן֙ לְרָחֵ֣ל בִּתּ֔וֹ אֶת־בִּלְהָ֖ה שִׁפְחָת֑וֹ לָ֖הּ לְשִׁפְחָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: To whom her father gave Bala, for her servant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Laban antoi tyttärellensä Rakelille piikansa Bilhan, piiaksi.
verses.king_james: And Laban gave to Rachel his daughter Bilhah his handmaid to be her maid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tandemque potitus optatis nuptiis, amorem sequentis priori prætulit, serviens apud eum septem annis aliis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא֙ גַּ֣ם אֶל־רָחֵ֔ל וַיֶּאֱהַ֥ב גַּֽם־אֶת־רָחֵ֖ל מִלֵּאָ֑ה וַיַּעֲבֹ֣ד עִמּ֔וֹ ע֖וֹד שֶֽׁבַע־שָׁנִ֥ים אֲחֵרֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And having at length obtained the marriage he wished for, he preferred the love of the latter before the former, and served with him other seven years.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän makasi myös Rakelin kanssa, ja piti Rakelin rakkaampana kuin Lean; ja palveli häntä vielä toiset seitsemän ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: And he went in also unto Rachel, and he loved also Rachel more than Leah, and served with him yet seven other years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns autem Dominus quod despiceret Liam, aperuit vulvam ejus, sorore sterili permanente.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤רְא יְהֹוָה֙ כִּֽי־שְׂנוּאָ֣ה לֵאָ֔ה וַיִּפְתַּ֖ח אֶת־רַחְמָ֑הּ וְרָחֵ֖ל עֲקָרָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord seeing that he despised Lia, opened her womb, but her sister remained barren.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska Herra näki Lean katsottavan ylön, teki hän hänen hedelmälliseksi; mutta Rakel oli hedelmätöin.
verses.king_james: And when the Lord saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb: but Rachel was barren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ conceptum genuit filium, vocavitque nomen ejus Ruben, dicens : Vidit Dominus humilitatem meam : nunc amabit me vir meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֤הַר לֵאָה֙ וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֔ן וַתִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖וֹ רְאוּבֵ֑ן כִּ֣י אָֽמְרָ֗ה כִּֽי־רָאָ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ בְּעׇנְיִ֔י כִּ֥י עַתָּ֖ה יֶאֱהָבַ֥נִי אִישִֽׁי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she conceived and bore a son, and called his name Ruben, saying: The Lord saw my affliction: now my husband will love me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Lea tuli raskaaksi, ja synnytti pojan, ja kutsui hänen nimensä Ruben, ja sanoi: Herra on katsonut minun ahdistukseni puoleen, nyt siis minun mieheni rakastaa minua.
verses.king_james: And Leah conceived, and bare a son, and she called his name Reuben: for she said, Surely the Lord hath looked upon my affliction; now therefore my husband will love me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rursumque concepit et peperit filium, et ait : Quoniam audivit me Dominus haberi contemptui, dedit etiam istum mihi ; vocavitque nomen ejus Simeon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֣הַר עוֹד֮ וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּן֒ וַתֹּ֗אמֶר כִּֽי־שָׁמַ֤ע יְהֹוָה֙ כִּֽי־שְׂנוּאָ֣ה אָנֹ֔כִי וַיִּתֶּן־לִ֖י גַּם־אֶת־זֶ֑ה וַתִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖וֹ שִׁמְעֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And again she conceived and bore a son, and said: Because the Lord heard that I was despised, he hath given this also to me: and she called his name Simeon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän taas tuli raskaaksi, ja synnytti pojan, ja sanoi: Herra on kuullut minua katsottavan ylön, ja on myös tämän minulle antanut: ja kutsui hänen nimensä Simeon.
verses.king_james: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Because the Lord hath heard that I was hated, he hath therefore given me this son also: and she called his name Simeon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Concepitque tertio, et genuit alium filium : dixitque : Nunc quoque copulabitur mihi maritus meus : eo quod pepererim ei tres filios : et idcirco appellavit nomen ejus Levi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֣הַר עוֹד֮ וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּן֒ וַתֹּ֗אמֶר עַתָּ֤ה הַפַּ֙עַם֙ יִלָּוֶ֤ה אִישִׁי֙ אֵלַ֔י כִּֽי־יָלַ֥דְתִּי ל֖וֹ שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה בָנִ֑ים עַל־כֵּ֥ן קָרָֽא־שְׁמ֖וֹ לֵוִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she conceived the third time, and bore another son, and said: Now also my husband will be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons: and therefore she called his name Levi.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän jälleen tuli raskaaksi ja synnytti pojan, ja sanoi: nyt taas minun mieheni pysyy minun tykönäni: sillä minä synnytin hänelle kolme poikaa. Sentähden hän kutsui hänen nimensä Levi.
verses.king_james: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Now this time will my husband be joined unto me, because I have born him three sons: therefore was his name called Levi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quarto concepit, et peperit filium, et ait : Modo confitebor Domino, et ob hoc vocavit eum Judam : cessavitque parere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֨הַר ע֜וֹד וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֗ן וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ הַפַּ֙עַם֙ אוֹדֶ֣ה אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה עַל־כֵּ֛ן קָרְאָ֥ה שְׁמ֖וֹ יְהוּדָ֑ה וַֽתַּעֲמֹ֖ד מִלֶּֽדֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: The fourth time she conceived and bore a son, and said: Now will I praise the Lord: and for this she called him Juda. And she left bearing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän tuli vielä raskaaksi, ja synnytti pojan, ja sanoi: nyt minä kiitän Herraa; sentähden kutsui hän hänen nimensä Juuda: ja lakkasi synnyttämästä.
verses.king_james: And she conceived again, and bare a son: and she said, Now will I praise the Lord: therefore she called his name Judah; and left bearing.
